sanskrit:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे॥
transliteration:
paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṃ vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām |
dharma-saṃsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge ||
literal_meaning: For the protection of the good and for the destruction of the evil-doers, for the sake of establishing dharma, I manifest Myself age after age.
contextual_meaning: The Self repeatedly embodies when inner goodness is threatened by destructive patterns, to restore psychological order and wholeness.
psychological_state: Empowered safety; trust that the psyche can self-correct and re-center when chaos threatens core values.
trigger_scenario: A therapist tells a trauma survivor that the mind has an innate drive toward healing and will keep bringing corrective experiences until balance is restored.
anchor_text: When inner order collapses, the Self re-appears to restore balance.
